entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The primary crop affected by sheath blight disease worldwide.

entities.name: SHEATH BLIGHT
entities.description: A disease caused by Rhizoctonia solani that threatens rice cultivation.

entities.name: RHIZOCTONIA SOLANI
entities.description: A basidiomycetous necrotroph pathogen that causes rice sheath blight.

entities.name: HIGH-YIELDING VARIETIES
entities.description: Rice varieties whose adoption increased the threat of sheath blight.

entities.name: NATURAL RESISTANCE
entities.description: The inherent ability of rice germplasm to resist the sheath blight pathogen.

entities.name: RICE GERMPLASM
entities.description: The genetic resources of rice used to search for natural resistance.

entities.name: RICE YIELD LOSSES
entities.description: The reduction in rice production caused by sheath blight disease.

entities.name: GLOBAL FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food, threatened by rice yield losses.

entities.name: GENETIC RESISTANCE
entities.description: Resistance developed through genetic means to combat the pathogen and avoid chemical fungicides.

entities.name: CHEMICAL FUNGICIDES
entities.description: Hazardous chemical treatments used to manage the sheath blight pathogen.

entities.name: HOST-PATHOGEN RELATIONSHIP
entities.description: The interaction between the rice plant and the Rhizoctonia solani pathogen.

entities.name: GENE MARKERS
entities.description: Loci or markers that impart a resistance response in the host.

entities.name: TRANSGENIC DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of modifying the host genome to introduce resistance.

entities.name: HOST GENOME
entities.description: The complete set of genetic material of the rice plant.
